howItWorks.summary: ASIRU coordinates all parties in the transaction. Buyer funds are placed in a letter of credit with a Russian bank, and once deal conditions are met, the bank settles with the payment partner.

caseStudies.title: Real deals, real outcomes
caseStudies.subtitle: How businesses across Southeast Asia close cross-border payments with Russian clients - in hours, not weeks.

items.title: Luxury villa deposit from Moscow
items.problem: A Phuket resort needed a ฿850,000 booking deposit from a Russian guest. International cards failed and P2P transfers were rejected by compliance.
items.solution: Asiru issued a QR invoice in minutes. The guest paid in rubles via SBP from their Russian banking app - no cards, no informal channels.
items.result: Deposit settled to the resort's Thai account the same evening. The booking was confirmed without losing the peak-season slot.

items.title: ฿12M condo down payment
items.problem: A Bangkok agency had 48 hours to receive a down payment from buyers in Russia. SWIFT was blocked and alternative brokers quoted unfavorable rates.
items.solution: We fixed the FX rate for 15 minutes, generated a compliant payment path, and provided full documentation for the agency's legal team.
items.result: Full down payment received within the contract window. Deal registered on schedule with transparent audit trail.

items.title: High-season restaurant group payments
items.problem: A restaurant group in Samui couldn't accept rubles from tour groups. Cash-only workarounds hurt revenue and created compliance risk.
items.solution: Each location got QR codes in the dashboard. Tour leaders paid group bills in RUB; settlements hit one THB account per venue.
items.result: Russian tourist spend increased 34% in one season. No POS hardware, no chargebacks.

items.title: Treatment package from St. Petersburg
items.problem: A wellness clinic needed upfront payment for a ฿680,000 program. The patient's Russian bank refused an international transfer to Thailand.
items.solution: Clinic staff created an invoice in THB. Patient scanned QR and paid in RUB with a locked rate shown on screen before confirming.
items.result: Treatment started on the planned date. Clinic received exact THB amount with fee breakdown for accounting.

about.title: More than a payment agent
about.description: Asiru is an international fintech team building regulated payment infrastructure between Russia and Southeast Asia. We operate within legal frameworks, with licensed partners and transparent processes - so your business can grow without payment friction.

items.question: How long does onboarding take?
items.answer: Most businesses are onboarded within 24 hours after submitting documents. Sign the agreement, provide company details, and start accepting rubles the next day.

items.question: How does the client pay?
items.answer: Clients scan a QR code or open a payment link in their Russian banking app. Payment goes through regulated bank channels - no cards or informal transfers required.

items.question: What currency do I receive?
items.answer: You receive funds in Thai Baht (THB) or USD to your local bank account, depending on your setup. The client always sees a clear amount in rubles.

items.question: Are there hidden fees?
items.answer: No. The exchange rate shown at invoice creation includes all fees. You receive exactly the amount you specified in your currency.

items.question: Is it safe and compliant?
items.answer: Yes. All payments flow through licensed banking partners and regulated infrastructure. We maintain full compliance with applicable financial regulations.

items.question: Who is Asiru for?
items.answer: Any business in Thailand or Southeast Asia that serves Russian clients - hospitality, real estate, retail, medical, travel, and more.
